name: Cowboy's Brisket
recipeIngredient: 4 lb First Cut Brisket Of Beef
recipeInstructions:
1. With a long thin, sharp knife, make slits in the meat and insert the slivers of garlic.
2. Place the meat in a bowl, spread 1 sliced onion over the meat, and add the crushed garlic and vinegar.
3. Marinate for 6 hours at room temperature or overnight in the refrigerator, turning several times.
4. When ready to cook, preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. Heat the bacon fat in a deep, heavy skillet large enough to hold the brisket over medium heat until shimmering.
6. Remove the brisket from the marinade and discard the onion and vinegar. Dry with paper towels.
7. Brown the meat well on all sides in the hot fat, working in batches if necessary.
8. Transfer the brisket to a platter. In the same skillet, sauté the remaining sliced onions until deeply browned.
9. Pour in 1/2 cup of coffee and bring to a boil, stirring and scraping the bottom of the skillet to loosen any browned bits.
10. Spread the onions and liquid from the skillet over the brisket in a shallow baking dish.
11. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper to taste. Pour in the remaining coffee and water.
12. Cover tightly with foil and place in the oven for 30 minutes.
13. Lower the temperature to 250°F (120°C) and bake for an additional 2 hours or until the meat is very tender.
14. Slice the brisket thinly against the grain. Skim the fat from the pan liquid.
15. Return the meat slices to the pan and serve immediately, or refrigerate for later use.
